What monomers link together to form prot: How many amino acids are commonly used to build proteins in humans?
Twenty standard amino acids.

What monomers link together to form prot: What functional groups are present on every standard amino acid?
An amino group and a carboxyl group, both attached to the central (alpha) carbon.

What monomers link together to form prot: What type of bond joins two amino acids in a protein?
A peptide bond, formed by a dehydration (condensation) reaction.

What monomers link together to form prot: What is the primary structure of a protein?
The linear sequence of amino acids in the polypeptide chain.

What monomers link together to form prot: What are the two most common types of protein secondary structure?
The alpha helix and the beta pleated sheet.

What monomers link together to form prot: What mainly stabilizes protein secondary structure?
Hydrogen bonds between backbone atoms.

What monomers link together to form prot: What is denaturation of a protein?
The loss of a protein's three-dimensional shape, which typically disrupts its function.

What monomers link together to form prot: What is an enzyme?
A biological catalyst, usually a protein, that speeds up a chemical reaction without being consumed.

What monomers link together to form prot: How does an enzyme speed up a reaction?
By lowering the activation energy required for the reaction.

What monomers link together to form prot: What is the substrate of an enzyme?
The reactant molecule that an enzyme acts upon.

What monomers link together to form prot: What is the region of an enzyme where the substrate binds?
The active site.

What monomers link together to form prot: What are the monomers that make up carbohydrates?
Monosaccharides (simple sugars).

What monomers link together to form prot: What monosaccharide is the primary energy source for most cells?
Glucose.

What monomers link together to form prot: What is the storage form of glucose in animals?
Glycogen.

What monomers link together to form prot: What are the building blocks (monomers) of nucleic acids?

What monomers link together to form prot: What three parts make up a nucleotide?

What monomers link together to form prot: Which nitrogenous bases pair together in DNA?

What monomers link together to form prot: What sugar is found in DNA?

What monomers link together to form prot: What are lipids characterized by chemically?

What monomers link together to form prot: What molecule is the main energy currency of the cell?

What monomers link together to form prot: What is released when ATP is hydrolyzed to ADP?

What monomers link together to form prot: What is the fluid portion of blood called?

What monomers link together to form prot: What protein in red blood cells carries oxygen?
